items.title: Children and 75yo among dozens of homeless in major holiday town

children.content: A homeless survey conducted in one of WA's best-known holiday hotspots uncovers dozens of people sleeping rough, including a 75-year-old.

items.title: Mystery of seventh man unsolved, months after Chinese boat arrivals

children.content: Months after the arrival of six Chinese nationals in one of Australia's most isolated communities, questions remain about another man suspected of attempting to assist them.

image.altText: a graphic of people on a boat

items.title: Tour operator cops $330K in cancellations as cyclone relief arrives for businesses

children.content: A whale shark boat skipper says one-off payments for tour operators will make a "small difference", as ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle's aftermath drags on.

image.altText: A whale shark floats in clear water below a blue and white tour boat, with the name "Draw Card" on its bow.

items.title: Shock loss of Emily has friends saying 'get your moles checked'

children.content: Emily avoided the sun and "did everything right". No-one considered that a melanoma removed from her shoulder in 2022 had spread to her brain.

gallery.altText: A group of people in black and purple clothing sit together on a bench. Shirts read "Remembering Em."
Two young women take a selfie together, both smiling at the camera, each with one hand under her chin.

items.title: HAZMAT warning lifted after blaze at Perth battery recycling facility

children.content: A man is taken to hospital after a serious fire at a lithium-ion battery recycling facility in Perth's south-eastern suburbs which also sparked a HAZMAT emergency warning.

items.title: Canada has flipped the script on child care. Could Australia copy?

children.content: Australia definitely won't nationalise child care, even though the data suggests there's a strong case. Perhaps a more feasible idea would be to copy what Canada has done.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: WA considering separate stockpile of diesel amid fuel shortages

children.content: The WA government is considering creating its own "strategic stockpile" of diesel, saying the state's size and the reliance of the agriculture and resources industries on the fuel warrants a separate reserve.

image.altText: A hand on a diesel pump at a petrol station.

items.title: Record crowd for AFL Gather Round as SA premier warns of 'lite' interstate version

children.content: South Australia's premier says he is "trying to hold the line" as Gather Round negotiations continue, with the 2026 event attracting record crowds.

image.altText: People wearing football colours enter into a stadium with the words GATHER ROUND on a screen

items.title: 'Missed opportunity': Astronomers lament loss of telescope partnership

children.content: The Artemis II mission used space technology developed in Australia, but experts fear the axing of a multimillion-dollar telescope partnership may stifle innovation and astronomy.

image.altText: Five buildings, four of which are firing lasers from their rooftops, against a starry Milky Way backdrop.

items.title: Caravan park residents evicted after change of ownership

children.content: Reg Hoyling thought his park home in Albany was where he would spend the rest of his life, but a sale of the caravan park meant he was among the tenants evicted.

items.title: Calls for greater protections for Rottnest after Indigenous remains discovered

children.content: Aboriginal leaders call for greater protections for Rottnest Island after the discovery of Indigenous remains during construction work at the popular holiday resort last week.

image.altText: An aerial photo of the Bathurst Lighthouse in front of a series of premium tents at Rottnest Island.

items.title: 'Like a flea on an elephant's back': The unexpected rise of solar power

children.content: Solar power is forecast to overtake coal as the world's biggest source of electricity next year, and its growth shows no signs of slowing down. Behind its success is a little-known Australian inventor.

items.title: ASX slips as oil surges back above $US100 on US threat of Gulf blockade

children.content: Failed US-Iran peace talks send oil prices surging higher and the Australian share market lower. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: 3D printed oil barrels and rising stock graph

items.title: How to make your workplace dog-friendly

children.content: How can employers design dog-friendly workplaces if they want to, and what does the law say about animals at work?

image.altText: A woman pats a dog sitting next to her at her desk at a workplace

items.title: Rory McIlroy joins exclusive group with second straight Masters victory

children.content: Rory McIlroy becomes just the fourth man in history to win back-to-back Masters titles after holding off a star-studded field in the final round at Augusta. Follow live.

articles.title: Day rues missed opportunities after golden chance at the Masters

children.content: Australia's Jason Day stays positive after falling to 12th on a difficult final day at the Masters.

items.title: How recycling can help solve a 'huge issue' in tennis

children.content: What should be done with all those used tennis balls that get inevitably sent to landfill? Lilian Xu might have found the answer.

items.title: Bondi Junction security guard who confronted attacker awarded bravery medal

children.content: Two years on from the Westfield Bondi Junction stabbing attack, eight heroes have been recognised in a special Australian Bravery Decorations honours list.

items.title: Firefighter encouraging more women to join front line
